name: Sour Cream and Wild Rice Soup
recipeIngredient: 2 Chicken Breasts, excess fat and skin removed
2 cups Wild Rice, Cooked (without fat or salt)
recipeInstructions:
1. In a heavy saucepan, combine the chicken and water. Bring to a boil.
2. Reduce heat to low and carefully skim off any scum. Add onion, carrot, celery, bay leaf, peppercorns, and salt.
3. Cover and simmer the chicken for 45 minutes to one hour. Remove the chicken from the broth and set aside to cool.
4. Strain the remaining broth, discarding the solids, and place in the refrigerator to chill or use a fat separator to remove all fat from the broth.
5. When the chicken is cool enough to handle, remove the meat from the bone and tear or shred into bite-sized pieces. Cover and refrigerate until needed.
6. Meanwhile, heat the olive oil in a large saucepan until sizzling. Add onions, celery, carrots, and garlic and cook over medium-high heat for about 5 minutes.
7. Lower the heat to medium, add mushrooms and cook for 5 more minutes.
8. Add flour, salt, and pepper and stir to combine, cooking for 2 minutes.
9. Add chicken stock (removing any accumulated fat) and evaporated skim milk.
10. In a small bowl, whisk together sour cream and cornstarch. Stir into the soup until thickened and heated through.
11. Add cooked rice, shredded chicken, and thyme, stirring until thick and bubbly.
12. Serve at once.
